description: Roles rooted in family ties, blood bonds, and kinship structures.
roles: Parent
A binding thread to home and history, giving the protagonist something deeply personal to fight for -- or against.
Sibling
Shared origin yet divergent path, creating a bond laced with rivalry, loyalty, or both in unpredictable measure.
Child
The future made tangible, making every danger feel heavier because what the protagonists are building toward is watching.
Grandparent
Old truths and forgotten debts surfacing through a living link to the past, revealing how history still shapes the present crisis.
Uncle/Aunt
A different lens on the protagonist's family, sometimes unlocking secrets or alliances the parents never would have offered.
Cousin
Familiar enough to trust yet distant enough to surprise -- a peer who can challenge assumptions or conspire in equal measure.
In-Law
Competing loyalties bridging two families, forcing the protagonists to navigate obligations they never chose but cannot escape.
Step-Family
Chosen bonds tested against the weight of blood, creating friction and tenderness that neither side fully expects.
Godparent
Sacred promise existing outside bloodlines but cutting just as deep, introducing duty that transcends mere kinship.
Ancestor
A voice reaching from beyond death to deliver warnings, blessings, or burdens the protagonists never asked to inherit.
